original: John Sharples of England finishes 371 hours of disco dancing
Circus ownerP. T. Barnumbuys his world-famous elephant Jumbo
Pixar Animation Studios (Toy Story; The Incredibles) headed by Edwin Catmull and Alvy Ray Smith, is spun off from Lucasfilm, Ltd as an independent film production company, with backing ofSteve Jobs
Henry Heimlich, Surgeon and inventor of the Heimlich maneuver, born in Wilmington, Delaware (d. 2016)
Plane crash known as "The Day the Music Died" kills musiciansBuddy Holly, Ritchie Valens, J. P. Richardson (aka The Big Bopper), and the pilot near Clear Lake, Iowa
Coldest wind chill likely ever recorded in the US of minus 108 degrees, taken at Mount Washington Observatory, New Hampshire
-81°F (-63°C), Snag Yukon (North American record)
Albert Spalding invests $800 to start a sporting goods company, manufacturing the first official baseball, tennis ball, basketball, golf ball, and football
Ibuprofen launched in the UK under brand name Brufen as prescription medicine - now world's most popular anti-inflammatory painkiller
Samuel Clemens first uses the pen nameMark Twainin a Virginia City newspaper, the "Territorial Enterprise"
George Adamson [Baba ya Simba - "Father of Lions"], British conservationist (Born Free), born in Etawah, India (d. 1989)
J Fred Muggs, a chimpanzee, becomes a regular on NBC's Today Show
Dr. Hook & the Medicine Show's single "The Cover of the 'Rolling Stone'", written by Shel Silverstein, enters the top 40 and peaks at #6
Norman Rockwell, American artist and illustrator (Saturday Evening Post covers), born in New York City (d. 1978)
First soft landing on the Moon, by the Soviet spacecraft Luna 9
10th NASA Space Shuttle Mission (41B): Challenger 4 launches
Moscow, Russia has its heaviest snowfall in a day on record (43 cm / 17 inches), killing one and bringing down 2,000 trees
Wilhelm Mauseth skates world record 500 m (46.8 secs)
Bartolomeu Diasand his crew are the first Europeans to land on South African soil at Mossel Bay (east of modern Cape Town)
Walt Disney's "3 Caballeros" released

PaleoanthropologistDavidson Blackreports his findings on the ancient human fossils found at Zhoukoudian, China, in the journal Nature and declares them to be a new species he names 'Sinanthropus pekinensis' (now known as 'Homo erectus')
Super Bowl XLII, University of Phoenix Stadium, Glendale, AZ: New York Giants beat New England Patriots, 17-14; MVP:Eli Manning, New York, QB
Super Bowl XXXVI, Louisiana Superdome, New Orleans, LA: New England Patriots beat St. Louis Rams, 20-17; MVP:Tom Brady, New England, QB
Canada beats United States, 6-1 to retain the Olympic ice hockey gold medal (won at 1920 Summer Games) at the Chamonix Games; Canadian LW Harry Watson top scores with 46 points
"Y.M.C.A." by Village People peaks at #2 on pop singles chart
New Zealand's worst natural disaster, the Hawke's Bay earthquake, kills 256 and injures thousands, devastating Napier and the Hawke's Bay region
John Uelses pole vaults record 489 cm
1st operational weather satellite, ESSA-1 launched US
Gro Harlem Brundtland is elected as the first female Prime Minister of Norway
Baseball's National League announces Yanks' broadcaster Bill White will its 1st African American President
Dam in Townsville, Australia, deliberately opened flooding 20,000 homes after city receives record 3.3ft of rain in a week
Canada's original Parliament buildings in Ottawa burn down
Prince Mutsuhito, 14, becomes Emperor Meiji of Japan (1867-1912)
"Meet the Beatles" album goes gold
Larry HolmesTKOs Lorenzo Holmes in 6 for heavyweight boxing title
Hennie Kuiper, Dutch road cyclist (Olympic gold road race 1972; world professional road race champion 1975; Tour de France 1977, 80 runner-up), born in Denekamp, Netherlands
Helen Stephens, American athlete (Olympic gold 100m & 4x100m relay 1936), born in Fulton, Missouri (d. 1994)
Charanjit Singh, Indian field hockey captain (Olympic gold 1964, silver 1960), born in Una, Himachal Pradesh, India (d. 2022)
Dave Davies, British rock guitarist and vocalist (The Kinks - "You Really Got Me"; "Lola"; "Living On A Thin Line"), born in Muswell Hill, North London, England[1]
Jim Marshall, American photographer (The Beatles final concert in San Francisco; Woodstock Festival), born in Chicago, Illinois (d. 2010)
Jen Bartlett, Australian tennis player, wildlife photographer and documentarian (The Flight of the Snow Geese), born in Wahroonga, Australia (d. 2025)
Johnny "Guitar" Watson, American R&B and funk guitarist ("Space Guitar"; "A Real Mother for Ya"), born in Houston, Texas (d. 1996)
Dennis Edwards, American pop and R&B singer (The Temptations - "Papa Was A Rollin' Stone"), born in Fairfield, Alabama (d. 2018)
Lee Crystal [Sackett], American drummer (Joan Jett & The Blackhearts - "I Love Rock'n'Roll"), born in Brooklyn, New York City (d. 2013)
Simone Weil, French philosopher, Resistance fighter (WWII) and social activist, born in Paris, France (d. 1943)
Columbia Shuttle moves to Vehicle Assembly Building at Kennedy Space Center (Florida) for mating for STS-3 mission
STS-60 (Discovery) launches into orbit
US PresidentRonald Reaganannounces formation of commission of enquiry on Challenger Accident
Geraldine McCullough wins Widener Gold Medal for Sculpture
Henry of Navarre (futureHenry IV) escapes from Paris
Jimi Hendrixadds vocals and overdubs for "Purple Haze" at Olympic Studios in London; basic track had been recorded by the band in January
The Pope andMother Teresameet in Calcutta
Public memorial held forCarl Saganat Cornell University, Ithaca, NY
Pope Francisarrives in Abu Dhabi, United Arab Emirates, on the first ever papal visit to the Arabian peninsula
Martin Dihigo  and John "Pop" Lloyd are elected to Baseball Hall of Fame
Black & Puerto Rican students boycott NYC public schools
6th largest snowfall in NYC history (17.4 inches or 44.2cm)
Early ice hockey game played between teams from Swavesey and Over on Mare Fen, England
Wisconsin Supreme Coutt declares US Fugitive Slave Law unconstitutional
Orbiting Solar Observatory 2 launches into Earth orbit (552/636 km)
